faqs.question: Are yellow jackets common in Jackson Heights gardens and parks?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jackson Heights' abundant gardens, parks like Travers Park, and mature trees provide ideal habitats for yellow jackets. They often nest in wall voids, under eaves, in attics, or underground. Late summer through fall is peak season when colonies are largest and most aggressive.

faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use targeted treatments that are safe for multi-family buildings, co-ops, and apartment complexes common in Jackson Heights. Our technicians are experienced in working around residents, pets, and shared spaces while effectively eliminating yellow jacket threats.
